raw_input_text:
Back-of-house (color mixing, stock, sterilization, maintenance): Handled loosely by owners/stylists (no dedicated BOH staff). Product stock (natural cosmetics from site) organized but no routines—mixing areas sometimes cluttered post-rush, sterilization basic (UV/disposables, but no logs), equipment (dryers, sinks) shows wear after 3 years (looks like 10, per details). No standards for checks/upkeep, leading to gradual decay.
FOH / Front-of-house snapshot
Reception + floor: No dedicated admin/receptionist (can't afford, per ops). Owners + tenants rotate duties loosely. Equipped solidly (mirrors, chairs, lighting, product displays). But undermanaged:

No full-time front desk → phones/emails/bookings handled ad-hoc, occasional delays (5–10 min waits for check-in).
Stations sometimes left with clippings/tools 4–7 min after clients.
Retail program undeveloped (products from site available, but no push—displays dusty, no sales training).
Minor gaps: “What’s the wait time?” → “Let me check with everyone” (no central schedule visibility). Social media/marketing absent (no strategy, content planning, brandbook/values unclear).
Scattered reviews/FB comments echo light frictions: “Amazing color but booking was confusing”, “Talents great, but salon could be tidier”, “Love the products but nobody explained them”.

coverage_gaps: No financial data provided — revenue, costs, margins, payroll percentage entirely unknown
No information on food safety or hygiene rating (EHO equivalent for Lithuania)
Staff turnover rate and tenure not mentioned
No detail on booking volume, capacity utilisation, or appointment fill rate
Employment contracts and legal compliance for tenant stylists not addressed
No information on pricing strategy or whether prices have been reviewed
Customer retention and repeat visit rate not discussed
No detail on supplier relationships for cosmetics products
Insurance and liability coverage not mentioned
No information on whether any formal training or certification exists for stylists

unmapped_observations.evidence: Owners + tenants rotate duties loosely. No dedicated BOH staff. Handled loosely by owners/stylists.
unmapped_observations.observation: Tenant-based staffing model creating accountability vacuum — no single person responsible for standards

unmapped_observations.evidence: 'What's the wait time?' → 'Let me check with everyone' (no central schedule visibility). 'Booking was confusing' (customer review). Phones/emails/bookings handled ad-hoc.
unmapped_observations.observation: Booking system absent or non-functional — no central schedule visibility causing customer-facing confusion

unmapped_observations.evidence: Retail program undeveloped (products from site available, but no push — displays dusty, no sales training). Customer review: 'Love the products but nobody explained them.'
unmapped_observations.observation: Retail cosmetics programme present but entirely passive — products stocked but not sold
Sterilization logs absent in a personal care/beauty context — this is a regulatory compliance risk, not merely an operational gap

unmapped_observations.evidence: Equipment (dryers, sinks) shows wear after 3 years (looks like 10, per details). No standards for checks/upkeep, leading to gradual decay.
unmapped_observations.observation: Accelerated physical decay of premises and equipment relative to age — suggests systemic maintenance neglect with compounding cost risk

unmapped_observations.evidence: 'Amazing color but booking was confusing.' 'Talents great, but salon could be tidier.' 'Love the products but nobody explained them.'
unmapped_observations.observation: Customer experience quality is stylist-dependent, not system-dependent — positive reviews praise individual talent, negative reviews cite systemic failures

unmapped_observations.evidence: No dedicated admin/receptionist (can't afford, per ops).
unmapped_observations.observation: Affordability constraint explicitly cited as reason for operational gaps — owner acknowledges structural resource limitation
